user_message: Before my call with Contoso Ltd, assess the risk. I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 14:12 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-01-06 11:45 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-03-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is migration architecture.

[00:06:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:14:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:19:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:22:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
